bead.title: ci: emit go test -json across the test suite and upload as artifact for timing visibility
bead.description:
PROBLEM
  Today no test runner emits durable timing data. lefthook pre-push,
  pre-commit, Makefile test target, and the GitHub Actions ci.yml
  full-suite step all run `go test ./...` and emit elapsed-time only to
  stdout. There is no JUnit, no `go test -json`, no aggregated artifact,
  no dashboard. The result: when a test gets slow (e.g.
  TestOSExecutor_EarlyCancel becomes a 60s test instead of a 10s test),
  the regression goes unnoticed until a developer manually inspects
  pre-push output. Splitting into unit / integration / e2e tiers is hard
  without baseline timing data.

CURRENT STATE
  - .github/workflows/ci.yml runs `go test -race -cover ./...` and
    emits stdout only.
  - .github/workflows/ci.yml uploads two artifacts (e2e-test-output,
    prompt-size-report) but neither is structured timing data.
  - No `go test -json` invocation anywhere in the repository.
  - Makefile has no test-with-json target.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add a CI step (in .github/workflows/ci.yml, in the existing test
    job or a new test-timing job) that runs:
        cd cli && go test -json -race -cover ./... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-output.json
    Then upload /tmp/test-output.json as a GitHub Actions artifact named
    `test-timing-<run-id>.json` retained for at least 30 days.
  - Optionally add a small Go program under cli/tools/test-timing/ that
    parses go-test-json events and emits a top-N-slowest-tests Markdown
    summary; write the summary as `test-timing-summary.md` and upload
    alongside.
  - Document in CLAUDE.md: "CI uploads test timing as a JSON artifact;
    use it to investigate slow-test regressions."
  - Do NOT modify the lefthook hooks — local pre-push timing is not
    durable and a JSON dump there would just clutter the developer's
    machine.

NON-SCOPE
  - Building a test-timing dashboard or a regression-detection alarm
    (separate concern; this bead just produces durable data).
  - JUnit format (the audit recommended JSON; JUnit is a separate
    concern if test-result reporters are needed).
  - Any change to `go test` invocation in lefthook.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Independent of all sister beads. Composes with the perf-tag and
    -short-gate beads (this bead measures; those beads optimize).
bead.acceptance:
1. .github/workflows/ci.yml contains a step that runs go test -json on the full suite (or a dedicated job that does so) and uploads the JSON output as a GitHub Actions artifact.
2. The artifact retention is at least 30 days (verify in the upload-artifact step config).
3. (Optional but encouraged) cli/tools/test-timing/ contains a small parser that emits a top-N-slowest-tests Markdown summary, also uploaded as an artifact.
4. CLAUDE.md or docs/helix/06-iterate/ documents the convention and where to find the artifact.
5. The CI job completes successfully on a test PR and the artifact is downloadable.
6. cd cli && go test ./... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
